system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (5 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (5 0 0);
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {

    }

    {
        type            slip;
    }

    {

    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;

    wedge
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    {

}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - lower region below wedge
    (-0.15242  0        -0.005)  // 0
    ( 0          0        -0.005)  // 1
    ( 0          0.09144  -0.005)  // 2
    (-0.15242  0.09144  -0.005)  // 3

    (-0.15242  0         0.005)  // 4
    ( 0          0         0.005)  // 5
    ( 0          0.09144   0.005)  // 6
    (-0.15242  0.09144   0.005)  // 7

    // Block 1 - upper region above wedge
    ( 0          0.09144  -0.005)  // 8
    ( 0.3048     0.09144  -0.005)  // 9
    ( 0.3048     0.1524  -0.005)  // 10
    ( 0          0.1524    0.005)  // 11

    ( 0          0.09144   0.005)  // 12
    ( 0.3048     0.1524    0.005)  // 13
    ( 0.3048     0.1524  -0.005)  // 14
    ( 0.3048     0.1524   0.005)  // 15
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (60 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (9 10 14 13)
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (8 12 14 9)
            (9 13 15 10)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type symmetryPlane;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }

    wedge
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Analysis

### 1. `log.blockMesh` — Face topology conflict

**Error:**  
`face 0 in patch 2 does not have neighbour cell face: 4(8 12 14 9)`

This is the same persistent error pattern. The face `(8 12 14 9)` is being reported as having no neighboring cell. Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`:

- Block 0: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` — lower-left block
- Block 1: `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)` — upper-right block

The `top` patch has two faces:
- `(8 12 13 9)` — bottom face of block 1 (should be internal/shared with block 0?)  
- `(9 13 15 10)` — top face of block 1

The error says face index 0 of patch 2 (`top`), which is `(8 12 14 9)`, has no neighbor. This face connects vertices 8(0,0.09144,-0.005), 12(0,0.09144,0.005), 14(0.3048,0.1524,0.005), 9(0.3048,0.09144,-0.005). This is a **diagonal face** that cuts across the interior of the computational domain — it's not a face of either block!

The problem is that **the face ordering in the `top` patch is incorrect**. Looking at the vertex coordinates:
- Vertices 8,9 are at z=-0.005 (bottom layer of block 1)  
- Vertices 12,13,14,15 are at z=+0.005 (top layer)

The two horizontal faces of block 1 are:
- Bottom face: `(8 9 10 11)` — at z=-0.005  
- Top face: `(12 13 14 15)` — at z=+0.005

But the `top` patch lists `(8 12 13 9)` and `(9 13 15 10)`. These are **cross-faces** connecting bottom to top across the block, which don't exist as boundary faces.

## Root Cause

The `top` patch face definitions are fundamentally wrong. The faces listed are not the actual top/bottom faces of the blocks — they are diagonal cross-faces that don't correspond to any physical boundary.

## Solution

### For `system/blockMeshDict`:

1. **Correct the `top` patch face definitions** to use the actual horizontal faces of each block:
   - For block 0 (lower block), the top face is at z=+0.005: vertices `(0 3 6 7)` — but this is partially blocked by the wedge. Actually, block 0's top face is `(3 2 1 0)`? No — let me trace properly.

   Looking at block 0 vertices:
   - Bottom face (z=-0.005): `(0 1 2 3)`  
   - Top face (z=+0.005): `(4 5 6 7)`

   Looking at block 1 vertices:
   - Bottom face (z=-0.005): `(8 9 10 11)`  
   - Top face (z=+0.005): `(12 13 14 15)`

   So the `top` patch should contain the **top faces** of both blocks:
   - Block 0 top face: `(4 5 6 7)`  
   - Block 1 top face: `(12 13 14 15)`

   But wait — vertex 7 is at (0, 0.09144, 0.005) and vertex 6 is at (-0.15242, 0.09144, 0.005). These are shared with block 1's bottom face vertices 8 and 9. So face `(4 5 6 7)` is actually the **interface between block 0 and block 1** — it should be an **internal face**, not a boundary face.

   Similarly, face `(12 13 14 15)` is the top face of block 1 and should be a boundary face.

   The correct `top` patch should only include the top face of block 1: `(12 13 14 15)`. The top face of block 0 is internal.

2. **The `bottom` patch** currently has `(0 1 5 4)`. This connects vertices across both blocks and is also an internal face — it should not be a boundary face. The true bottom boundary is the bottom face of block 0: `(0 1 2 3)`.

3. **The `frontAndBack` patch** currently has `(0 3 2 1)` and `(4 5 6 7)`. These are the bottom and top faces of block 0, which are internal faces (shared with block 1 or the wedge). They should not be boundary faces.

4. **Summary of correct boundary patches**:

   - `inlet`: `(0 4 7 3)` — left vertical face of block 0 at x=-0.15242 ✓
   - `outlet`: `(9 10 14 13)` — right vertical face of block 1 at x=0.3048 ✓
   - `top`: `(12 13 14 15)` — top face of block 1 ✓
   - `bottom`: `(0 1 2 3)` — bottom face of block 0 ✓
   - `wedge`: `(3 7 6 2)` — wedge-facing face of block 0 ✓
   - **Remove `frontAndBack`** — there are no remaining external faces not already covered ✓

The core issue is that the `top` and `bottom` patches contain cross-block faces that are internal, not external boundaries. Only the exposed outer faces should be boundary patches.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change top patch face list from [(8 12 13 9)(9 13 15 10)] to [(12 13 14 15)]; Change bottom patch face list from [(0 1 5 4)] to [(0 1 2 3)]; Remove the frontAndBack patch entry entirely'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 5 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
